prompt: What does 'high-level' actually mean in OCP's rule that 'higher-level components must be protected from changes in lower-level ones'? Some teammates say it means more generic / more reused; others say it means closer to business policy. Which is right? Give a concrete example of each interpretation applied to the same codebase.

assertions.text: Picks the 'closer to business policy' interpretation as correct
Defines 'level' as distance from I/O / from the system's input-output edges (NOT genericness or reuse count)
Provides concrete examples: business-rule class as high-level vs heavily-reused utility as low-level
Explains protection direction: keep DB/UI/framework churn from climbing up into business rules
Explicitly cautions against conflating reuse-count or genericness with levelness
